stories.title: Throwback Thursday 1973 Buffalo Bills season highlights
stories.summary:
2 letters. OJ



	 



	As we open a brand new stadium, we look back at the opening season for Rich Stadium.  An absolute palace compared to the dilapidated War Memorial Stadium on Best Street in downtown Buffalo. Simpson led the League in rushing the previous year and Lou Saban kept adding bruising OL to bolster the rushing game. Side notes: I was in my seat in time to see Herb Mul-key return the initial KO in preseason for a TD because my uncle was a cop and his buddies in OP opened a path allowing us to race to the stadium and I celebrated my 18th birthday attending the Eagles game vs Roman Gabriel -yes, I’m very old.. with the Electric Company, we turned on the Juice! I’ll never forget how excited Van Miller got when Juice was hemmed in deep in the backfield vs the Colts only to see OJ slip, dip and scoot 70+ yards for a TD! Rookie QB Joe Ferguson had replaced Dennis Shaw and we went on a tear culminating in 3000 rushing yards and OJ’s 2003 yard season!



	 



	Enjoy!



	 



	
		
	



	
	Our week 7 game -our 1st ever MNF game. My dad -still with us at 93, took me to this one as well!

stories.title: Peterman goes from player to agent in new path
stories.summary:
Peterman is expected to become a certified NFL agent within the next few months.



	 



	“I got to live through what I’m now getting tested on in the CBA,” he said of his new career path. “Being up and down on a roster, being put on IR, negotiating contracts. I hope I can help a younger person that’s on a similar journey.”



	
	https://nypost.com/2026/06/03/sports/former-nfler-nathan-peterman-goes-from-player-to-agent-in-new-path/



	
	How the hell did he play 6 years on an NFL team? I wish him luck. Maybe he’ll be the Tom Brady of agents.

stories.title: The Sporting News proposes massive Bills/Vikings trade.......would ya??????
stories.summary:
I honestly don't see how this trade could work Salary Cap-wise, and I also don't see the Vikings accepting this offer unless Jefferson forces it, but here it is:



	 



	Bills get: WR Justin Jefferson



	 



	Vikings get: 2027 1st, 2nd, and 4th rounder



	                     2028 3rd and 5th rounder



	 



	Here's the article this came from:



	 



	https://sports.yahoo.com/articles/bills-vikings-massive-trade-pitch-225131883.html

stories.title: Deone Walker “scared” for what Ed Oliver can do to opponents in Jim Leonhard’s scheme
Deone Walker Interview from OTA
stories.summary:
Not much changed from what I recall how he talked last year and the Eric Wood podcast earlier this year. But I do feel the young man's confidence, compared to the other guys interviews (Sanders, Jackson, etc.). I think he knew he can play in NFL. What I want to see is how good he can be. Hopefully his ceiling is higher than Jordan Philips who a lot of people compare him to.



	 



	One interesting tidbit is he mentioned Leonhard wants to rotate guys including positions (1, 3, 4i) so an offensive lineman doesn't always face the same guy. I'm not sure I buy that. There are about 60-70 defensive snaps to be played in a game and the rotation of 6 guys can only do so much. You have to have different technique against the same offensive lineman.

stories.title: General Sabres Media Info & Coverage
Add a Good Not Great Player to Our Current Squad
stories.summary:
Excluding Legends, you can bring back any player from the Bills, in his prime with us. But he has to be:



	 



	5 years retired from football (theoretically HOF eligible)



	 



	Not in the Pro Football Hall of Fame 



	 



	Not on the Bills wall of Fame. 



	 



	My initial answer was Kyle Williams. But I feel that it's only a matter of time before his name goes up on the wall.

stories.title: TBDAHOT: Rollcall
stories.summary:
I understand things may look drastically different this year, but I want to get a sense who’s up for keeping this tradition going!



	 



	Please let me know if you want to get together for the home opener this year. It doesn’t matter if you’ve gone to 1, 25 or if you have never attended one. Everyone’s welcome! Even if you don’t plan on going inside for the game.



	 



	With regards to this topic, I do not have any interest in people who are going and plan to het together elsewhere. Enjoy yourself, be safe, but do not talk about it in this topic.



	 



	I don’t want to officially speak for others, but I believe me, Jay Rosen, and Guffalo are definitely carrying on the tradition. 



	 



	Who else?

stories.title: Buchanan, Smith named finalists for NLL Awards
stories.summary: Buffalo Bandits forwards Dhane Smith and Kyle Buchanan have been named finalists for the NLL’s end-of-season awards, the league announced Wednesday.   Smith was tabbed as a finalist for both NLL MVP and Offensive Player of the Year while Buchanan is up for the NLL’s Sportsmanship Award.   It’s the second consecutive season both players are finalists for their respective awards as Smith is the reigning Offensive […]

stories.title: Gurts Fast Break: We’ll Be Back
stories.summary: Another Season Not good at long goodbyes, but I want to write that it has been a wonderful run with the Bandits, including the five recent NLL Finals appearances and the team’s back-to-back-to-back championships. Last Saturday’s defeat to the Georgia Swarm was devastating, given the rungs on the ladder climbed this season to earn a […]

stories.title: Bandits’ season ends with 1st-round loss to Georgia
stories.summary: Lyle Thompson had a game-high 10 points and Nolan Byrne had a hat trick in his playoff debut as the Georgia Swarm eliminated the Buffalo Bandits from the 2026 NLL Playoffs with a 17-10 win at Gas South Arena on Saturday. Thompson tallied four goals and six assists to set a playoff career-high in points […]

stories.title: How a team meeting turned the tide on the Bandits season
stories.summary: The Bandits entered the 2025-26 season the kings of the NLL universe with three consecutive championships. That uncertainty continued into early February as a 3-1 start was followed […]
